page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d1ne.8xlarge is a storage-focused virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance provides 32 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, resulting in 32 vCPUs. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. A defining characteristic of this instance is its massive local storage capacity, consisting of 94,489 GB of local HDD storage. Additionally, it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

The hardware profile presents distinct performance tradeoffs. While the 94,489 GB HDD storage capacity provides exceptional density for large-scale data retention, the underlying HDD technology is optimized for sequential throughput rather than the high random IOPS typical of solid-state drives. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance from the Intel Xeon processor without resource contention. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the integration of massive local HDD storage alongside a balanced CPU and memory configuration makes this instance highly cost-effective for workloads requiring high storage capacity per instance without the premium cost of SSD-based storage arrays.

Given its massive local HDD capacity and balanced compute resources, the ecs.d1ne.8xlarge is engineered for storage-intensive workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale data warehousing, distributed file systems, big data analytics platforms like MapReduce or Hadoop, and cold data storage. It also fits log processing and data archiving tasks that require high sequential read and write operations across massive datasets.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d1ne.8xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM and a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. The defining feature of this KVM-based instance is its massive local storage capacity of 94,489 GB on HDD media. This configuration offers a high-density storage profile with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The hardware design presents a tradeoff, prioritizing massive sequential storage capacity over high random IOPS. This makes the instance highly cost-efficient for large-scale data processing and retention. It is best suited for big data analytics, distributed file systems, data warehousing, and archival storage workloads.

tagline: A high-density storage instance featuring dedicated processing cores and massive local HDD capacity for large-scale data processing and archival workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core

Optimized for big data analytics, distributed file systems, and data warehousing
